content.intro: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks verify your identity as a landlord. PropertyGoose tracks AML status and alerts you when checks need to be renewed.

sections.title: AML Status Types
sections.content: Your AML status shows as: 'Not Requested' (no check has been initiated), 'Requested' (check is pending), 'Satisfactory' (check passed), or 'Unsatisfactory' (check failed or concerns identified).

steps.title: Open the landlord record
steps.content: Go to Landlords and click on the landlord you want to check.

steps.title: Initiate AML check
steps.content: Click the AML verification button to start the check process. The landlord will receive a verification request.

steps.title: Landlord completes verification
steps.content: Follow the link to verify your identity. This typically involves providing ID documents and confirming personal details.

steps.title: Review results
steps.content: Once complete, the AML status will update to 'Satisfactory' or 'Unsatisfactory'. View the full report in the landlord's profile.

sections.title: AML Renewal
sections.content: AML checks should be renewed every 2 years. PropertyGoose shows a warning banner when your AML check is outdated. Click 'Re-check AML' to initiate a new verification.

sections.title: Bulk AML Status
sections.content: The Landlords page shows the 'Date AML Completed' column. Entries with checks older than 2 years will be flagged for attention.
